message:
fix(tests): remove memory leak in TestFixture command execution

Remove dangerous `Box::leak` usage in the integration test fixture's
command execution logic. The previous implementation leaked memory by
converting error messages to static string references, which would
accumulate over multiple test runs.

Changes:
- Replace `Box::leak(format!(...).into_boxed_str())` with simple
  `format!(...)` returning an owned String
- Add explicit type annotation `Result<std::process::ExitStatus, String>`
  to clarify the loop's return type
- Convert static string "Command timed out" to owned String via
  `.to_string()` for consistency
- Simplify final error branch to use the String directly instead of
  calling `.to_string()` on an already-owned String

The `Box::leak` pattern is rarely appropriate and creates memory that
is never deallocated. In test code running many iterations, this could
cause memory exhaustion. The fix uses standard String ownership which
is properly cleaned up when the Result goes out of scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
